conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your inquiry. To help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children for whom you'd like to claim Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex, who's 17, and Blake, who's 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess eligibility for Alex and Blake, I need to ask a few more questions.

Let's start with Alex, who is 17. Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They finished school but haven't continued with anything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Just to clarify about Alex - is Alex currently working? And if so, how many hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One more question about Alex - is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't receiving any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I have a few questions about your general circumstances:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Do both Alex and Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Just one final question - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**For Blake (age 3):** ✅ **You are eligible**
Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

**For Alex (age 17):** ❌ **You are not eligible**
Unfortunately, you don't qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. While Alex is under 20, the rules state that for children aged 16 or over, they must be in approved education or training to continue receiving Child Benefit. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in education, training, or an apprenticeship, you cannot claim Child Benefit for them.

**Important note:** If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit for them again at that point, as long as they remain under 20.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Blake. If you have any questions or if Alex's circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has finished education but is not currently in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit continues for children aged 16 or over only if they stay in approved education or training and are under 20. Since Alex is not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, even though Alex is not working and not claiming benefits in their own right.

Blake is 3 years old, which is under 16. The claimant lives in the UK, Blake lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. The claimant is responsible for Blake as they live together. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (17) and Blake (3). They live in the UK with both children and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. For Blake, who is 3 years old, the claimant meets all eligibility criteria as Blake is under 16 and lives with them. However, for Alex, who is 17, the claimant is not eligible because Alex is not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for children aged 16 or over to qualify for Child Benefit.
